name: Inducible Trf2 deletion caused telomere uncapping and vascular dysfunction in mice
summary: TRF2 loss-of-function supports the causal importance of telomere capping in vascular aging biology.
statement: Inducible Trf2 deletion in adult mice increased arterial telomere uncapping and p21 senescence signaling while impairing vasodilation, microvascular perfusion, endothelial glycocalyx thickness, and blood pressure regulation.
population_or_model: Adult mice with tamoxifen-inducible Trf2 deletion
quantitative_note: The source reports 55% greater arterial telomere uncapping, 320% higher arterial senescence signaling, 29% and 22% lower peak vasodilation in carotid and mesenteric arteries, 63% lower glycocalyx thickness, 8% lower perfusion, 9% higher systolic blood pressure, and 210% higher arterial superoxide.
caveats: This is a loss-of-function disease model, not a therapeutic restoration study.
Whole-body Trf2 deletion is an acute genetic perturbation and may not model gradual human aging cleanly.
